creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #5)

I'll note that the 4 GiByte RPi4B Rev 1.1 that I used has the
EEPROM content identified by:

RPi: BOOTLOADER release VERSION:8ba17717 DATE: 2023/01/11 TIME: 17:40:52

In other words, from:

https://github.com/raspberrypi/rpi-eeprom/releases/tag/v2023.01.11-138c0

[Warning, there is a duplicate that swapped the 11 vs. 01 in the date
encoding in the file name:

https://github.com/raspberrypi/rpi-eeprom/releases/tag/v2023.11.01-138c0 ]

Such could have an effect on the live adjustments made to the
device tree in .dtb used. (Adjustments made before even U-Boot
is involved, much less FreeBSD.)

I've no clue what the 4 GiByte RPi4B v1.2 reported on has for
its EEPROM content's version.
